explanations.2: The -e flag tells echo to interpret backslash escape sequences like \e[1;34m (which sets the text color to blue) and \n (which creates a newline).
explanations.3: Similarly, this line uses -e with echo to print colored text; \e[1;32m makes the text green, and \e[0m resets the color back to normal.
explanations.6: The --host 0.0.0.0 makes the server listen on all network interfaces (not just localhost), and --no-browser prevents Quarto from automatically opening a web browser.
